title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS backed by $85M+ in funding and trusted by brands like The New York Times and Wiz, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale its GTM operational backbone. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Excellent alignment across all dimensions: senior RevOps executive with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds the 7+ year requirement, Fintech domain matches strong fit, Salesforce expertise confirmed, and scaling experience well-positioned for $30M+ ARR growth. Tel Aviv hybrid location is ideal match.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's impressive $85M+ funding round and their trust from enterprise brands like The New York Times—exactly the type of high-growth, mission-critical SaaS that demands rigorous RevOps infrastructure. With your 15+ years scaling GTM operations in B2B SaaS and deep Fintech expertise, your background operationalizing complex, cross-functional motions at scale seems like a natural fit for building Finout's revenue backbone as it accelerates beyond $30M ARR.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager focused on territory strategy and operations. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory compositions, conducting whitespace analysis, and maintaining mapping in CRM systems like Salesforce and Fullcast. Requires a deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and CRM proficiency. Salary range $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS GTM mastery, and Salesforce proficiency. Territory strategy, pipeline optimization, and cross-functional collaboration with Sales/Finance are core RevOps competencies. Only minor gap: role is specialized in territory management rather than broader RevOps leadership.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building revenue operations engines in B2B SaaS. I've redesigned territory models for hypergrowth, optimized Salesforce for sales effectiveness, and partnered with Sales/Finance/Strategy on GTM scalability. Gong's focus on territory-driven revenue optimization aligns perfectly with how I think about RevOps—and I'm excited about the analytical rigor this role demands.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
description: Flex seeks a Revenue Operations Manager - Systems to build the New Verticals RevOps function. The role involves building and owning the Salesforce environment, designing revenue tech stack integrations (CRM, enrichment, routing), and architecting the sales process from lead-to-close. Requires 5+ years in Revenue/Sales/GTM Operations with 2+ years in a senior capacity, deep Salesforce expertise (Admin Certification preferred), and familiarity with GTM tools like Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot. Builder mentality essential.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds 5+ year requirement, senior RevOps background aligns perfectly with building new verticals function, deep Salesforce expertise and GTM tech stack knowledge (Outreach, LeanData, HubSpot) directly match job requirements, and builder mentality is core to senior RevOps profiles.
ai_opener: Omri - I came across Flex's Revenue Operations Manager - Systems role and immediately thought of you. Building a RevOps function from scratch for new verticals requires exactly the kind of Salesforce architecture expertise and GTM systems thinking you've honed over 15+ years in B2B SaaS. Your experience designing revenue tech stacks and lead-to-close processes would be instrumental in scaling Flex's sales operations.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Platomics, a healthcare/diagnostics tech company, seeks a part-time (20h/week) RevOps Manager with potential to move full-time in 2027. Responsibilities include administering the CRM (HubSpot), ensuring data hygiene and integrations, partnering with sales, marketing, and finance on revenue strategies, and supporting forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting. Requires 3+ years in revenue operations, sales operations, or business analytics, preferably in tech (SaaS, cloud, AI), and knowledge of forecasting methodologies and pipeline management. Salary €25,000–€31,000 annually.
score_reason: Strong technical and operational fit with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience, HubSpot expertise, and deep forecasting/pipeline knowledge. Concerns: part-time entry (20h/week) is below target seniority level, healthcare/diagnostics is outside core fintech/AI-native GTM focus, and €25-31k salary significantly undervalues senior executive profile.
ai_opener: Hi, I came across Platomics' RevOps Manager role and was impressed by your focus on data hygiene and revenue strategy in healthcare tech—an area where rigorous forecasting and CRM governance are critical. With 15+ years building GTM engines across B2B SaaS, including extensive HubSpot and Salesforce administration, pipeline forecasting, and quota architecture, I'd be a strong fit, though I'd like to discuss whether a part-time structure aligns with the strategic scope you're building.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks, an AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, seeks its first GTM Engineer to serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization. The role owns setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Nooks has grown ARR 4x then 3x in two years and is loved by sales teams at companies like HubSpot and Rippling. Ideal for a technically-minded operator who can optimize sales tooling and workflows.
score_reason: Excellent match for a senior RevOps executive seeking a GTM Engineer role at a high-growth AI sales platform. The technical optimization focus, sales tooling expertise, and B2B SaaS scaling context (4x then 3x ARR growth) align perfectly with Omri's core competencies and target roles.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Nooks and immediately thought of you—this GTM Engineer role feels like it was designed for a RevOps leader at your level. You've spent 15+ years optimizing sales workflows and revenue infrastructure in B2B SaaS, and Nooks is doing exactly that but embedded in an AI-native platform that's already resonating with HubSpot and Rippling. The chance to be the first GTM Engineer for a $70M+ backed platform growing 3-4x annually seems like the perfect inflection point to shift from managing RevOps functions to architecting them.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS, a B2B SaaS facility management platform for mid-to-large enterprises, seeks a GTM Engineer to build, configure, and improve AI agents across support, success, sales, and marketing. Working at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, you'll design how their 'AI brain' learns and operates in the real world, building the company's first AI Workforce of intelligent agents that help GTM teams work smarter and faster. Focus on both how AI systems connect/run and how they learn over time.
score_reason: Strong alignment with AI-native GTM focus and B2B SaaS enterprise context. However, the role emphasizes AI agent engineering/configuration over traditional RevOps strategy, which is adjacent but not perfectly centered on Omri's core GTM/revenue operations expertise.
ai_opener: Omri here—your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because you're building AI agents that directly optimize how revenue teams work. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM ops, I've seen firsthand how the right automation and data flows transform pipeline forecasting and team velocity. I'd be curious to discuss how you're approaching the learning layer of these agents—especially how they'll adapt to your customers' evolving sales workflows.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Dust, a Sequoia-backed company building an AI operating system for enterprises with 70%+ weekly active users, seeks a GTM Engineer. Serving customers like Cursor, Clay, Whatnot, and Persona, Dust aims to dramatically scale growth. The team comprises experienced operators from companies like Stripe and OpenAI who focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously. Ideal for a technical GTM operator excited about applying AI agents to go-to-market motions.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps/GTM expertise and AI-native company focus. GTM Engineer role at a well-funded, fast-scaling SaaS company with technical GTM challenges is ideal for Omri's profile, though the role emphasizes technical execution over revenue strategy leadership.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've been building GTM engines at B2B SaaS companies for 15+ years, and Dust's approach to applying AI agents to go-to-market motions is exactly where the space is heading. With your Sequoia backing and 70%+ WAU, you're at the inflection point where RevOps infrastructure and AI-assisted sales/marketing workflows become competitive advantages. I'd be excited to explore how to scale your GTM motion with technical rigor.

title: GTM Engineer
description: WorkOS builds modern developer tools and APIs that help companies become Enterprise Ready, powering authentication, identity, and authorization. Having raised a $100M Series C at a $2B valuation, WorkOS powers enterprise features for fast-growing AI companies including OpenAI, Cursor, Perplexity, Vercel, and Plaid. The GTM Engineer role focuses on technical go-to-market infrastructure at the frontier of human and agent authentication. score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native company focus, and technical RevOps infrastructure needs. Some misalignment: developer-tools B2B2B model differs from Omri's core enterprise SaaS experience, and role emphasizes technical builder skills over revenue operations strategy leadership.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across WorkOS and was impressed by how you're positioning authentication and identity as the GTM moat for AI companies—that's exactly the kind of infrastructure-first thinking that separates founders who scale from those who plateau. Given your 15+ years architecting RevOps systems and pipeline forecasting at scale, I thought you'd appreciate the challenge of building GTM infrastructure for a platform where technical enablement *is* the sales motion. Would love to explore if this GTM Engineer role could be the next evolution of your builder background.

title: Head of Sales and Marketing (B2B SaaS)
description: This remote-from-Israel role owns both sales and marketing strategies, spanning customer acquisition, revenue expansion, and retention. Responsibilities include developing a comprehensive GTM strategy, implementing an end-to-end sales process, optimizing marketing across SEO, paid media, content, and partnerships, and leading pricing/packaging experiments. Requires 7–10+ years in B2B SaaS covering both sales and marketing, proven success scaling SMB/mid-market revenue engines, deep SaaS metrics knowledge (ARR/MRR, CAC, LTV, NDR), and fluency in English and French. Fully remote with high autonomy.
score_reason: Strong alignment on RevOps/GTM strategy, SaaS metrics, and remote-from-Israel eligibility. Primary gap: role emphasizes sales/marketing dual ownership rather than pure RevOps, and French fluency requirement is unaddressed in profile.
ai_opener: Omri—I came across Jobgether's Head of Sales and Marketing role and thought of you immediately. Your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM strategies and deep expertise in pipeline forecasting, CAC/LTV optimization, and revenue operations map directly onto the end-to-end sales process and metrics-driven approach this role demands. I'd love to explore whether the sales/marketing blend aligns with where you want to focus next.

title: Head of Sales (Mid-Market B2B)
description: Belkins seeks a Head of Sales reporting to the Chief Growth Officer to lead the full sales organization (AEs, BDRs, Sales Operations) and drive the next phase of growth, targeting 30%+ new sales revenue growth in 2026. Responsibilities include coaching AEs, strengthening the outbound engine, elevating revenue operations, designing scalable processes for complex multi-stakeholder deals (ACVs up to $1M+), and partnering with Marketing as one GTM team. Ideal for a transformation-driven leader from SaaS, digital marketing, or consulting with data-driven sales expertise.
score_reason: Strong GTM/RevOps background and remote location are ideal, but this is a pure Head of Sales role (people leadership, quota-carrying) rather than a RevOps/Operations-focused position. Omri's expertise in pipeline forecasting, processes, and revenue operations is valuable, but lacks the direct sales team management and quota accountability this role demands.
ai_opener: Belkins' focus on elevating revenue operations and designing scalable processes for complex deals resonates with my RevOps background—I've spent 15+ years architecting GTM engines in B2B SaaS. While my strength is building the operational backbone that enables sales teams to scale, I'm curious whether you'd consider a RevOps leader to partner with your Head of Sales, or if you're open to someone bridging both skill sets.

title: Marketing Operations Manager
description: Cadonix, a global leader in cloud-delivered ECAD tools, seeks a Marketing Operations Manager to lead strategy, administration, and optimization of HubSpot as the core marketing and revenue operations platform.
